Convolve a function I with the codex filter at \_lambda transmissivity. For each available filter transmissivity T, computes the sum of
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{\_lambda}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}) \textendash{} filter center in Angstrom.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{I}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{callable}}) \textendash{} function to convolve, argument is lambda in Armstrong

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{verbose}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) \textendash{} print info about computation. Defaults to True.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
convolution between I(lambda) and the filter transmissivity

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
float

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar

\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{min\_x}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) \textendash{} integration limit for LOS away from observer in solar radii. Defaults to 10.

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Get the temperature by inverting CODEX images ratio.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{codex\_images}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{np.ndarray}}) \textendash{} images from CODEX measurements. Should be a {[}4, m, n{]} array containing 4 filters measurements {[}T1, S1, T2, S2{]} (in order) of shape {[}m, n{]}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{wind\_speed\_pos}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{np.ndarray}}) \textendash{} {[}m, n{]} array or float containing the wind speed intensity in the POS for each pixel of the CODEX measurements. If a float is provided, homogeneous wind speed is assumed.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{densities\_dc}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{np.ndarray}}) \textendash{} datacube of shape {[}l, m, n{]} containing electron densities in cm\textasciicircum{}\sphinxhyphen{}3. The first index contains values along the LOS for each pixel, and POS is assumed to be at int(l/2).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{helio\_distances\_dc}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{np.ndarray}}) \textendash{} datacube of shape {[}l, m, n{]} containing heliocentric distances of each density datacube entry in solar radii. The first index contains values along the LOS for each pixel, and POS is assumed to be at int(l/2).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{pid}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}) \textendash{} id used for printing progress.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
temperatures resulting from inversion in the POS, in Kelvin.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
np.ndarray

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{invert\_temperature\_parallel() (in module doppler\_dimming\_lib.inversion)@\spxentry{invert\_temperature\_parallel()}\spxextra{in module doppler\_dimming\_lib.inversion}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{doppler_dimming_lib:doppler_dimming_lib.inversion.invert_temperature_parallel}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{doppler\_dimming\_lib.inversion.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{invert\_temperature\_parallel}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{codex\_images}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{ndarray}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{wind\_speed\_pos}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{ndarray}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{densities\_dc}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{ndarray}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{coordinates\_dc}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{ndarray}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{total\_processors}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{int}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}{{ $\rightarrow$ ndarray}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Get the temperature by inverting CODEX images ratio. This version first splits the domain for X processors and then applies invert\_temperature\_batch to each subdomain, before merging the result.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{codex\_images}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{np.ndarray}}) \textendash{} images from CODEX measurements. Should be a {[}4, m, n{]} array containing 4 filters measurements {[}T1, S1, T2, S2{]} (in order) of shape {[}m, n{]}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{wind\_speed\_pos}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{np.ndarray}}) \textendash{} {[}m, n{]} array or float containing the wind speed intensity in the POS for each pixel of the CODEX measurements. If a float is provided, homogeneous wind speed is assumed.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{densities\_dc}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{np.ndarray}}) \textendash{} datacube of shape {[}l, m, n{]} containing electron densities in cm\textasciicircum{}\sphinxhyphen{}3. The first index contains values along the LOS for each pixel, and POS is assumed to be at int(l/2).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{coordinates\_dc}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{np.ndarray}}) \textendash{} datacube of shape {[}l, m, n{]} containing coordinates of each density datacube entry in solar radii. The first index contains values along the LOS for each pixel, and POS is assumed to be at int(l/2).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{total\_processors}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) \textendash{} number of processors for parallelization. This number should be tweaked to avoid asymmetric division of the domain which can increase the computation time. If set to None, 4 processors are used. Defaults to None.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
temperatures resulting from inversion in the POS, in Kelvin.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
np.ndarray

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

Returns the electron density or temperature from a Metis map fitted with DDT. For the electron temperature calculation see utls.T\_e\_analytical.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{param}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}) \textendash{} parameter to calculate, either Ne or Te

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{map\_los}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{sunpy.map.Map}}) \textendash{} map contaning the parameter and metadata in sunpy format

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{coefficients}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{np.ndarray}}) \textendash{} {[}8, 360{]} array containing the fitted coefficients in the first axis.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{pixel\_pos}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}) \textendash{} {[}y, x{]} pixel coordinates at which to calculate the parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{sun\_center\_pos}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}) \textendash{} {[}y, x{]} pixel coordinates of the sun center. Provided by the user to avoid useless calculations.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{x\_los}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}) \textendash{} distance in pixels along the line of sight at which to calculate the parameter.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
electron density at set location

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
float

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}
